title: Dialect Portability, NoSQL Tradeoffs, Distribution, and Production Defense · Data-System Defense
summary: A team must move a browser prototype toward PostgreSQL, justify any non-relational component, and defend the complete data system. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a production-ready cross-dialect data system portfolio. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate standard relational intent from SQLite and PostgreSQL syntax, typing, NULL, date, and feature behavior.
Choose relational, document, key-value, graph, or mixed models from access, integrity, evolution, and consistency needs.
Explain replication, partitioning, distributed transactions, consistency choices, and failure boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Dialect Portability · changed case · Quiz Dialect Portability
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Move a SQLite 3.49.1 quiz artifact toward PostgreSQL 18.4 while preserving relational intent. Record one dialect difference, consistency boundary, restore signal, and changed case 2; justify any non-relational component.

Which action demonstrates Dialect Portability without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Dialect Portability · changed case · Quiz Dialect Portability.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate standard relational intent from sqlite and postgresql syntax, typing, null, date, and feature behavior. For Dialect Portability · changed case · Quiz Dialect Portability, keep Dialect Portability connected to executable relational evidence.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Capstone Defense

Record one dialect difference, consistency boundary, restore signal, and changed case 4; justify any non-relational component.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend schema, queries, mutations, transactions, security, performance, portability, recovery, and ethical limits under changed evidence.
